# Transcrição: 03-3 Google Ads

- **Fonte:** 03-3 Google Ads
- **Duração:** 10:18
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

## [00:00 - 03:05]

Hey everybody, David O'Connell here, and today we're going to be talking about the psychology of Google Ads for direct response advertisers. We're going to get into techniques that we've used to create high-converting Google Ads, spend millions profitably, and techniques we've used to scale companies from a million dollars a year in revenue to over a hundred million dollars a year in revenue. So first up, I'm David O'Connell. I'm the founder and CEO of O'Connell Digital Media Group, and I've been running Google Ads for direct response advertisers for a little over 20 years now. I originally got involved with Google Ads back in 2003, and I was working with a language earning company. We were able to buy clicks back then for highly competitive terms for about ten cents each, which was an incredible time to be involved with Google Ads and learn how Google Ads work. The cost of Google Ads has risen dramatically over the past 20 years, but the effectiveness of them has not declined. And for top performing advertisers, Google is an incredible source of very large amounts of new customers that can be acquired profitably using Google Ads. As I mentioned, we have direct response focus. We specialize in building campaigns that profitably scale customer acquisition. And I currently lead a team of high-performance media buyers and customer acquisition experts. So the things that I'll be sharing with you today, these are in theory. These are things that are working now to scale real businesses. So the big picture with Google Ads, the reach of Google Ads is huge. Google Ads will reach over 90% of all internet users worldwide. It makes this platform incredibly scalable, and it's why so many direct response advertisers look to Google to accomplish their mass customer acquisition goals. It's very common to come to Google and acquire 50, 100, 200, 300 or more customers a day if you use all the platform and if you use the ad types right. The thing though is there are many Google ad types to choose from, and there's an incredible array of powerful target options. So if you don't know when and how to use each of them, it's really easy to get lost or overwhelmed. And in this training, we're going to cover all the major campaign types that apply to direct response advertisers and how direct response advertisers should use them. And we're also going to talk about how to make effective ads for each campaign type. So these are the five types, Google ad campaign types. First one up is search ads. I think everybody at some point in their life has gone to Google.com and done a Google type a keyword into the search engine and had search results come up and seen the text ads that are associated with the search ads. So these are primarily text ads with extensions, and these are served on the search engine results page for Google.com and their search partners.

## [03:05 - 06:05]

Second Google ad campaign type is shopping ads. These are click to buy now style ads, and they get served on Google shopping. They get served in the Google search engine results page, and they also get served in Google images, maps, and with search partner results. Next up we've got Google display ads, which are responsive image and text ads that get served across the Google display network. Next up we have demand generation. These are responsive image text and or video ads, and really as of April of 2025 demand generation has become really the powerhouse ad campaign type. The ads are currently being served on YouTube, Discover and Gmail of Google properties, or the Google display network, which is Google's aggregation of all their partner websites that they can serve ads of. And as of April 2025, Google ads has phased out conversion ads, conversion video ads as a standalone campaign type. And now if you're running any type of conversion objective oriented YouTube ads, they're going to be run under demand generation. So we've seen a huge shift in volume from YouTube campaign types to now moving into demand campaign types. Also as of April 2025 demand gen ads also serve in the Google display network. So previously these were focused on YouTube, Discover and Gmail, just Google owned properties. Now if you're having success on the Google owned properties, you can also expand into the Google display network and get huge amounts of reach. So this is becoming a really, really important campaign type. And one of the follow up modules here, we're going to get a lot deeper into how to use this campaign type. One of the really cool things about it too is you can be really successful here with video like you would have traditionally been with YouTube ads. You can also be really successful here with image and text ads. We'll talk more about that. And then finally there's performance Max ads. And performance Max ads are a combination of all the above ad types. So when you create a performance Max ad, you're typically taking your best ads from each of the above campaigns, loading them all in there. And then Google uses its AI to decide which ads to serve to who and where. It really opens you up to a lot more of Google's ad network. Okay, next up, let's talk about different campaign types for different business objectives. So when you look at your customers, a great way to look at them is by what stage of market

## [06:05 - 09:09]

awareness they're in using Eugene Schwartz terminology. So if you are looking for your most aware and your product aware customers, these are customers that already know about you and they're typically looking for a reason to buy or they're just looking to you. So they can buy and you just have to show up and look good. The campaign types you really want to lean most heavily on are going to be search, shopping and performance Max. These are very high return and ad spend channels because you're able to advertise directly to people who are already in buying mode and ready to buy. But there's limited volume here, right? Because there's only so many people that are already in that mindset and that have already been influenced by your advertising who are actually ready to buy. Then if we want to generate more demand, we want to generate more cold traffic volume, we need to look further up to less aware stages like people who are solution aware, problem aware, even people who might be problem unaware. And in those cases, the best campaign types to use are going to be demand generation or performance Max. And the potential of these are very, very high volume. Again, because you can reach 90% of the year. So one thing I did was take a look back here at a few different ad accounts over the last couple of months since the transition in April of 25 where YouTube has been phased out and where Google Display Network has opened up all their ad inventory within demand generation. And so I went and looked back across a few ad accounts. I think this represents about $5 million in spend over the last couple of months and looked at what the most common distribution by campaign type is, right? So this gives us a sense for which campaign types are most direct response advertisers using most heavily. And what we saw here is that the big heavy hitter, not a big surprise, is demand gen coming in at 58% of spend on average. Second up is performance Max at 27% of spend. Third up is search at 11% of spend. And then standard shopping campaigns coming in at 4% of spend. So again, keep in mind here that what was traditionally considered YouTube and traditionally considered display have both been rolled up under demand gen here, which is a big part of why this category has grown so much, so quickly. Usually I would say probably about half of this for most advertisers is video, half of this is image, but that varies from account to account, depending on the market they're in. We see some advertisers who just dominate all of this with images. We see other advertisers that dominate all this with videos using what would be considered

## [09:09 - 10:18]

traditionally great YouTube strategies. Performance Max, it's important to keep in mind that a lot of what gets served within Performance Max is also shopping ads. There's standard shopping ads here, which is about 4%. But I would also say usually for a physical product, about half of the Performance Max volume you get is going to be automatically serving search feed ads as opposed to display ads or search ads, etc. And then you can see you've got search here at 11%, shopping at 4%. As I mentioned, these tend to be the lower volume campaign types, but these tend to be very high return and ad spend campaign types. So they usually have a big impact in subsidizing the overall performance of the account and helping to drive higher row ads on top of the volume you get from demand gen and Performance Max. All right, so next up, who's ready to make some Google ads? We're going to have follow up modules here to go into each of the different Google campaign types and how to make effective ads for each campaign type. Stay tuned.
